summary_one_line: Take the false branch by supplying a well-formed sfnt header with a COLR table tag at offset 0x0c so get_glyph_extents succeeds for the base codepoint.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid OpenType/sfnt structure: the 00 01 00 00 sfnt version at offset 0x00, a literal 'COLR' four-byte table tag at offset 0x0c, and consistent table-directory offset/length fields so font->get_glyph_extents returns true (non-zero) for buffer->info[base].codepoint, taking the negated false branch at line 328.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the exact byte run 00 01 00 00 00 01 ... 43 4f 4c 52 ('COLR') at offsets 0x00-0x0f and stable directory bytes (e.g. 00 00 00 18, 01 ee 7f 0a), giving a parseable glyph store; the W role hits line 335 onward and runs position_mark, while line 328 shows W T=0 F=20 (always false = extents succeed).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are unstructured garbage (e.g. runs of bf bf bf, 3d 3d '==', 37 37 '7') with no COLR tag at 0x0c, so get_glyph_extents fails, the true branch is taken (L 328 T=27 F=0), and execution falls into zero_mark_advances and returns early.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which plain value_profile lacks: the per-execution CmpLogObserver captures the magic-number / tag comparisons (sfnt version, 'COLR' tag) and I2SRandReplace substitutes the exact constant operands into the input, planting the required literals at their offsets so get_glyph_extents succeeds. Plain value_profile has only Hamming/prefix-distance bucket feedback (no operand substitution), so it can register being 'close' to the tag but cannot place the exact 4-byte literal, and stays blocked all 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the negated extents call; the false (resolved) side requires get_glyph_extents to succeed for the base codepoint.
Winner resolves all 10 trials while loser is blocked all 10, an I2S-delta pair.
Winner seeds contain a valid sfnt header and the literal COLR table tag at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
The COLR tag and header bytes are stable across winner seeds (consistent byte sets at the tag offsets).
Loser-blocking seeds are unstructured garbage with no COLR tag, e.g. runs of bf and 3d bytes.
At the blocker line the winner role always takes false (extents succeed) while loser always takes true (extents fail).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 328  T=0 F=20  T=27 F=0  if (!font->get_glyph_extents (buffer->info[base].codepoint,  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser execution falls into zero_mark_advances after extents fail, winner runs position_mark instead.
value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace while value_profile does not, the only mechanism delta in this pair.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace plus the per-execution CMP buffer over plain value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5671 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00722b1acd0aaeac` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depth 34 of the 35-level ancestor chain; tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 34', the lower-bound floor signal for an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on get_glyph_extents success in which value_profile resolves the false branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog despite needing the exact 4-byte 'COLR' tag literal — showing the CMP_MAP gradient alone, without I2S operand substitution, suffices to plant the literal.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the decisive comparison I2S substitutes is the 'COLR' tag specifically versus the sfnt version or another table-directory constant — the byte diff shows multiple stable literal offsets and the exact CMP that gates extents success is inferred, not directly observed.
